schema.name: TZPID source truth promoted from reviewed L2 recovery candidate
schema.basis: reviewed D-drive source evidence; local candidate preview code bleed stripped

dictionary_sections.definition: Anomalous Diffusion in Cymatic Fields names the nonlocal diffusion behavior that appears when a cymatic carrier is modeled by a stochastic acoustic field equation rather than a purely local wave equation.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In the registry arc, the entry marks the point where cymatic resonance stops being only a pattern description and becomes a dynamical field law: the fractional Laplacian carries long-range diffusion, while nonlinear and stochastic terms describe field self-coupling, thermal agitation, and jump activation.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation supports nonlocal spreading, stochastic activation, and nonlinear cymatic self-interaction. It also gives the derivation lane a clean separation between wave propagation, anomalous diffusion, Kerr response, thermal noise, and jump processes.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: Fractional Laplacian; Riesz potential; anomalous order; Kerr nonlinearity; stochastic cymatic field evolution.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: ID1262 is grounded in the stochastic cymatic field evolution packet: standard wave propagation is supplemented by a nonlocal fractional Laplacian, Kerr-type nonlinearity, thermal noise, and jump activation.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: Grounded in the publish reassembly source lines for fractional/anomalous operators and ID 852 stochastic cymatic field evolution.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry repairs the local code-bleed packet by replacing shell-command residue with the actual source-backed anomalous diffusion equation.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus: anomalous diffusion; cymatic field; fractional Laplacian; stochastic acoustic evolution; Kerr nonlinearity.
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: Mathematically, ID1262 is a stochastic partial differential equation with a nonlocal fractional Laplacian. Its symbolic support belongs to fractional calculus, stochastic analysis, nonlinear wave dynamics, and cymatic control.

publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: Code-bleed support removed; source-backed fractional/cymatic equations inserted.

wolfram.note: Carrier form preserves source LaTeX as Wolfram-readable data; semantic proof remains domain-specific.

theory.canonical_statement: The cymatic field evolves by a stochastic wave-flow equation whose drift includes a fractional Laplacian term. That fractional term represents anomalous diffusion inside the acoustic/cymatic carrier rather than ordinary local diffusion alone.
theory.technical_interpretation: ID1262 is grounded in the stochastic cymatic field evolution packet: standard wave propagation is supplemented by a nonlocal fractional Laplacian, Kerr-type nonlinearity, thermal noise, and jump activation.

reviewed_source_symbols.meaning: fractional Laplacian term carrying anomalous diffusion
cubic/Kerr nonlinearity coefficient for cymatic field self-interaction
thermal diffusion/noise scale in the stochastic field equation
Poisson jump increment for phonon activation
